subject: SAR · Fri Jul 17 — 10 races, 4 flags, 6 passes

pick_notes.1:
Maiden fields are the hardest races to price, and we're leaning on the figure here. Beijing tops our number at 22% in the turf sprint, a notch above where the board has him. Not a strong statement — maidens rarely are — but the figure says he's live and the price is generous.

Fair 7/2, play 4/1 or better. The thinner side of a thin race: a bet at the number, not a chase.
pick_notes.5:
Six horses want the lead in this five-and-a-half turf dash — a pace meltdown waiting to happen. Boston's Phinest is the one our figures like to be standing when it clears: a 16% shot where the board has him nearer 8, a nine-point gap and the biggest number we're giving up on a longshot today.

It's a price with a reason. Fair 5/1, play 6/1 or better. If the speed burns itself out the way six-deep usually does, this is the horse it sets up for.
pick_notes.8:
Our widest disagreement at Saratoga today. Ice Chocolat is the top figure in the turf route, and we make him a 19% chance where the board has him at better than double that price — a nine-point gap. Even pace, so no traffic excuse either way; the number just likes the horse.

Fair 4/1, play 9/2 or better. About as straightforward as our flags get — the figure says he's underpriced, and you only collect if you hold out for 9/2.
pick_notes.9:
The last flag of the day is a longshot in the De La Rose. Deep Satin tops our figures in the mile turf stakes at 13% — not a number that screams, but the board's is quieter still, and three-deep early speed gives an off-pace type something to run at late.

Fair 13/2, play 15/2 or better. A dart at a stakes price: one unit if you can get the number, nothing if you can't.
